title: Flip
description:
Implement `flip(a, i)`, which reverses a length-$i$ vector:

$$\text{flip}(a,i)_k = a_{i-1-k}$$

**Input**: `a` shape `[i]`, `i` (python int, the length, equal to `a.shape[0]`). **Output**: shape `[i]`.

Solve with broadcasting/index arithmetic only, no `torch.flip`.
starter_code:
import torch

def flip(a, i):
    # Return a reversed: out[k] = a[i-1-k]. No torch.flip.
    pass
hints: Solve in 1 line using broadcasting only.
torch.arange(i - 1, -1, -1) directly builds the reversed index vector [i-1, i-2, ..., 0].

solutions.name: Descending arange index
solutions.explanation: `torch.arange(i - 1, -1, -1)` produces the index vector $[i-1, i-2, \dots, 0]$; indexing $a$ with it reads $a_{i-1-k}$ at every position $k$, reversing the vector.
